key_drivers: Fed policy remains a dovish hold, but the dollar firmed modestly on the day, creating a mixed cross-asset backdrop.
Risk-off volatility is elevated with the VIX above 25 and rising, which is bearish for broad equities but supportive for gold and yen.
The Bank of Japan maintained a tightening bias and the RBA delivered a March hike, preserving Asia-Pacific FX divergence versus the Fed.
World Gold Council data showed another month of gold ETF inflows (+US$5.3bn in February), while EIA data showed a 6.9 million-barrel crude build.

stale_sources: DFII10 and HY OAS official series were one to two trading days stale at the latest available official print.
World Gold Council ETF flows are the latest monthly data through February 2026 rather than intraday March 26 flow data.
Eurozone growth input was not directly re-verified during this run and was therefore held neutral.
fallbacks_used: TradingView structured page data for DXY, MOVE, and SOX daily direction.
Investing.com quote-page fields to classify copper direction.
Accessible Reuters and World Gold Council article text for BoJ stance and gold ETF flow direction.
overnight_changes: VIX rose 8.65% on the day and remained above the risk-off threshold of 20.
SOX was positive on the day while MOVE fell materially, helping offset some of the Nasdaq headwind from the broader risk backdrop.

imminent: U.S. GDP final estimate due March 26
near_term: Next EIA weekly petroleum report on April 1
Next major U.S. labor data in early April
background: Middle East conflict and oil-price shock risk
Potential near-term BoJ follow-through after the March hawkish hold
RBA post-hike repricing in AUD rates
